summary: The suite covers all major requirements: empty queue, all-success, transient failure, permanent failure/DLQ routing, retry count accuracy, progress-reporter callbacks, mixed-item summaries, ToSummary output, and InMemoryQueue FIFO behaviour. BaseDelayMs=0 across every test correctly prevents slow test runs. Moq is used well — processor and reporter are properly mocked, while InMemoryQueue is used directly as a deterministic collaborator. The Q() helper reduces boilerplate. Main gaps: (1) The exponential-backoff delay formula (BaseDelayMs × 2^(N-1)) is never exercised — setting BaseDelayMs=0 skips all Thread.Sleep paths, so a broken delay calculation would go undetected. (2) MaxRetries=0 (only the initial attempt allowed) is untested. (3) Progress-report field accuracy (ProcessedSoFar and FailedSoFar counters inside emitted events) is not verified — tests only count how many events of each status were emitted. (4) The internal DLQ code path (no external DLQ injected) has no dedicated test. (5) Test 9 ToSummary uses Assert.Contains("2", summary) which is trivially satisfied by multiple occurrences of the digit '2' and provides weak confidence in formatting correctness.
